visualization.description1: • The graph is generated by the backend's `feature_visualization.py` helpers and rendered with vis.js.
visualization.description2: • Schema level reveals available schemas; table level highlights foreign-key relationships between tables.
visualization.description3: • Column level maps column-level dependencies based on foreign keys.
visualization.description4: • You can click and drag nodes, zoom in/out, and pan with your mouse.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Learn how to use SQLSphere effectively
docs.gettingStartedDesc: Quick guide to get you up and running
docs.connectionsDesc: Learn how to connect your databases securely
docs.dataImportDesc: Import data from various file formats
docs.tipsDesc: Get the most out of SQLSphere
docs.welcomeText: SQLSphere is your intelligent database assistant that helps you explore, query, and visualize your data using natural language. No need to remember complex SQL syntax – just ask questions in plain English!
docs.step1Desc: Sign up for free to access all features and save your connections.
docs.step2Desc: Connect your database securely using credentials or SSH tunnel.
docs.step3Desc: Ask questions about your data in natural language.
docs.step4Desc: View database structure and relationships visually.
docs.standardConnectionDesc: Direct connection to your database using Host, Port, Database Name, Username, and Password. Ensure your database is accessible from this machine.
docs.sshTunnelConnectionDesc: Connect to a database behind a bastion/jump host via SSH. Useful for cloud databases or private networks. SQLSphere establishes an SSH tunnel to the jump host and forwards the database port through it.
docs.localSocketDesc: Connect to a database running on the same server using a Unix domain socket file instead of TCP/IP. Available for PostgreSQL and MySQL. Faster and more secure for local connections. Provide the socket file path (e.g., /var/run/postgresql/.s.PGSQL.5432).
docs.namedPipeConnectionDesc: Connect to SQL Server using a Windows Named Pipe instead of TCP/IP. Only available on Windows when both the client and server are on the same machine or domain. Provide the pipe name (e.g., \\.\pipe\MSSQL$SQLEXPRESS\sql\query).
docs.localAgentConnectionDesc: The Local Agent is currently under development. Once available, it will let you connect to a database on your local machine via a lightweight desktop app — securely and without port forwarding.
docs.localhostWarningText: You are using 'localhost' as the host. If SQLSphere is deployed on a server, you cannot directly access your local database.
docs.localhostWarningTextMySQL: You are using 'localhost' as the host. If SQLSphere is deployed on a server, you need to use an SSH tunnel to access your local database.
docs.sshTunnelDesc: If you want to connect to a local database (localhost) from a deployed website, you need to use an SSH tunnel.
docs.sshTunnelStep1: **Enable SSH Tunnel** - Select 'SSH Tunnel' as the connection method in the connection dialog.
docs.sshTunnelStep2: **Enter SSH Host** - Enter the IP address or hostname of your computer where the database is running (e.g., your public IP or a DynDNS hostname).
docs.sshTunnelStep3: **SSH Credentials** - Enter your SSH username and password to connect to your computer.
docs.sshTunnelStep4: **Database Host** - Use 'localhost' or '127.0.0.1' as the database host, as the connection goes through the SSH tunnel.
docs.sshTunnelStep5: **Port Forwarding** - The SSH tunnel automatically forwards the database port from your local computer to the server.
docs.sshTunnelNote: Note: Your computer must be accessible from outside via SSH. Make sure port 22 (SSH) is open in your firewall.
docs.sshTunnelSetupTitle: Setting up SSH on your computer
docs.sshTunnelSetupDesc: To use SSH tunnels, you need to enable SSH server on your local computer and make it accessible from outside.
docs.sshTunnelSetupMac: **macOS:** SSH is installed by default. Enable it in System Settings → General → Sharing → Remote Login. Or in Terminal: `sudo systemsetup -setremotelogin on`
docs.sshTunnelSetupWindows: **Windows:** Install OpenSSH Server via 'Settings → Apps → Optional Features → OpenSSH Server'. Or use WSL with SSH.
docs.sshTunnelSetupLinux: **Linux:** SSH is usually pre-installed. If not: `sudo apt-get install openssh-server` (Ubuntu/Debian) or `sudo yum install openssh-server` (CentOS/RHEL).
docs.sshTunnelSetupCredentials: **SSH Credentials:** Use your regular computer username and password. On macOS/Linux, this is your system username. On Windows, you can create a separate SSH user.
docs.sshTunnelSetupIP: **Find Public IP:** Visit https://whatismyip.com or run `curl ifconfig.me` in terminal. Important: This IP may change (dynamic IP).
docs.sshTunnelSetupDynDNS: **DynDNS (recommended):** For a fixed address, use a free DynDNS service like No-IP, DuckDNS, or Dynu. This keeps your address the same even if your IP changes.
docs.sshTunnelSetupFirewall: **Firewall:** Open port 22 (SSH) in your router firewall (port forwarding) and in your computer firewall. On macOS: System Settings → Network → Firewall → Firewall Options.
docs.sshTunnelSetupRouter: **Router Configuration:** Set up port forwarding for port 22 from your router to your local IP address. Find your local IP with `ipconfig` (Windows) or `ifconfig` (macOS/Linux).
docs.connectionTipText: Always test your connection before saving. This ensures your credentials are correct and the database is reachable.
docs.connectionUI1: **Add Connection Button** - Opens the connection dialog. Requires login. Free plan is limited to 1 connection; Pro plan allows unlimited connections.
docs.connectionUI2: **Database Type Dropdown** - Select PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQL Server. The port, SSL options, and available authentication methods adjust automatically.
docs.connectionUI3: **Connection Method** - Choose from five options: 'Standard TCP/IP' (direct network connection), 'SSH Tunnel' (via bastion/jump host), 'Unix Socket' (MySQL/PostgreSQL local socket file), 'Named Pipe' (SQL Server on Windows), or 'Local Database (Agent)' (via the SQLSphere desktop agent).
docs.connectionUI4: **Authentication Method** - Choose how to authenticate. Options vary by database: Standard SQL credentials, Windows Authentication (SQL Server), Azure AD — Password / Service Principal / Integrated / MFA / Managed Identity (SQL Server & PostgreSQL), AWS RDS IAM (MySQL & PostgreSQL), SSL Certificate Auth, or Custom Connection String (expert mode).
docs.connectionUI5: **SSH Tunnel Fields** - Shown when 'SSH Tunnel' is selected: SSH Hostname, SSH Port (default 22), SSH Username, SSH Password, and optional SSH Key File path. The database host/port fields still refer to the database server as seen from inside the SSH host.
docs.connectionUI6: **Local Agent Section** - Shown when 'Local Database (Agent)' is selected: click 'Generate Code' to create a unique connection code, then enter it in the SQLSphere Agent app on your local machine together with your database credentials.
docs.connectionUI7: **Host, Port & Named Instance** - Enter the database server address and port. For SQL Server you can optionally provide a Named Instance (e.g., SQLEXPRESS) instead of a port.
docs.connectionUI8: **Database & Credentials** - Enter the database name, username, and password. Fields adapt based on the chosen authentication method (e.g., hidden for Windows Auth; replaced by Azure/AWS settings for cloud auth).
docs.connectionUI9: **SSL / TLS Configuration** (collapsible section) - Set the SSL Mode (PostgreSQL: disable/allow/prefer/require/verify-ca/verify-full; MySQL: disabled/preferred/required/verify_ca/verify_identity). Optionally provide CA Certificate, Client Certificate, and Client Key via file path or paste PEM. SQL Server has a separate Encrypt dropdown and Trust Server Certificate toggle.
docs.connectionUI10: **Default Schema** - Optional: pre-select a schema used in chat and visualization (e.g., 'public' for PostgreSQL, 'dbo' for SQL Server).
docs.connectionUI11: **Test Connection Button** - Sends the credentials to the backend to verify the connection and caches the session. Always test before saving to ensure everything is correct!
docs.connectionUI12: **Save / Edit / Delete** - Save stores the connection for future use. The Edit icon (pencil) reopens the dialog with existing values. The Delete icon (trash) permanently removes the connection.
docs.tooltipDbType: Select your database system (PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQL Server)
docs.tooltipHost: Server address (e.g., localhost or IP address)
docs.tooltipDbName: Name of the database to connect to
docs.tooltipSsl: Enable SSL for encrypted connection (recommended for remote)
docs.tooltipDefaultSchema: Optional: Set a default schema to use
docs.tooltipTestConnection: Test if the connection works before saving
docs.tooltipSaveConnection: Save this connection for future use
docs.tooltipConnHost: Enter the database server address (e.g., localhost, 192.168.1.100, or domain name)
docs.tooltipConnPort: The port your database listens on. Default: PostgreSQL=5432, MySQL=3306, SQL Server=1433
docs.tooltipConnDatabase: The specific database name you want to connect to
docs.tooltipConnPassword: Your database password. Stored securely and encrypted.
docs.tooltipConnSsl: Enable SSL/TLS encryption for secure connections (recommended for remote databases)
docs.tooltipConnTest: Verify that the connection works before saving
docs.aiFeature1Desc: Ask questions like 'Show me all users who signed up last month' and get results instantly.
docs.aiFeature2Desc: The AI generates optimized SQL queries based on your natural language questions.
docs.aiFeature3Desc: Get insights and summaries about your query results automatically.
docs.aiFeature4Desc: With the modifications toggle enabled, the AI can execute INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, DROP statements directly. Changes are shown as diffs in the editor with Keep/Reject buttons.
docs.exampleQuery1: Show me the top 10 customers by total orders
docs.exampleQuery2: What were our sales last quarter?
docs.exampleQuery3: Find all products with low inventory
docs.exampleQuery5: How many active subscriptions do we have?
docs.aiTipText: Be specific in your questions! Instead of 'show data', try 'show the last 20 orders with customer names and total amounts'.
docs.chatUI1: **3-Column Layout** - The interface is divided into: **Schema Browser** (left, 20%), **SQL Editor** (middle, 40%), and **AI Chat** (right, 40%). All panels are resizable.
docs.chatUI2: **Database Dropdown** (top left) - Select which database connection to use for this chat session.
docs.chatUI3: **Chat Sessions Card** (top right) - Shows all your previous chats. Click to switch between them. Edit (pencil) to rename, delete (trash) to remove.
docs.chatUI4: **Schema Browser** (left panel) - Browse all database objects (tables, views, procedures, functions, triggers, sequences). Click tables/views to open SELECT query in editor. Right-click for context menu.
docs.chatUI5: **SQL Editor** (middle panel) - Multi-tab editor with syntax highlighting, autocompletion, and diff view. New queries open in new tabs. Modifications show diffs with Keep/Reject buttons.
docs.chatUI6: **AI Chat** (right panel) - Natural language interface. Type questions and get SQL generated automatically. Shows compact diff (red/green lines) for changes. Keep/Reject buttons to accept or discard AI suggestions.
docs.chatUI7: **Message Input** (bottom of chat panel) - Auto-resizing textarea. Type your question in natural language. Press Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line.
docs.chatUI8: **Send Button** - Submits your question to the AI. The AI generates SQL, opens it in the editor, and executes it automatically.
docs.chatUI9: **Allow Modifications Checkbox** - When enabled, the AI can execute INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, DROP statements directly (no confirmation required).
docs.chatUI10: **Query Results Panel** (bottom, 50% height) - Shows all executed queries and their results. Horizontally scrollable tables. New results appear at the top and auto-scroll.
docs.schemaBrowser1: **Schema Folders** - Expandable folders showing each schema in your database. Click the '>' button to expand/collapse.
docs.schemaBrowser2: **Tables/Views/Materialized Views** - Click a table/view name to open 'SELECT * FROM table' in a new editor tab (no implicit LIMIT is added). Click the '>' button next to the name to expand and see all columns with their data types.
docs.schemaBrowser3: **Procedures/Functions** - Left-click to view the source code definition in the editor. Right-click for context menu: 'Execute Procedure', 'View Definition'.
docs.schemaBrowser4: **Triggers/Sequences** - Right-click for context menu options: 'View Definition', 'Delete'.
docs.schemaBrowser5: **Refresh Button** (circular arrow, top) - Reloads the schema if you made database changes.
docs.schemaBrowser6: **Context Menu** (right-click) - Available for all objects: 'Select Top 100 Rows', 'View Definition', 'Execute Procedure' (for procedures), 'Delete'.
docs.schemaBrowser7: **Column Display** - Tables, views, and materialized views show columns with data types when expanded. Click the small arrow button (>) next to the object name to expand/collapse columns.
docs.schemaBrowser8: **Horizontal Scrolling** - The schema browser is horizontally scrollable if content is wider than the panel.
docs.queryPanelDesc: The Query Results Panel (bottom, 50% height) shows all executed queries and their results. New results appear at the top and auto-scroll. Tables are horizontally scrollable.
docs.queryPanelFeature1: View all executed queries and their results
docs.queryPanelFeature2: New results appear at the top automatically
docs.queryPanelFeature4: Get AI help when your SQL has errors (automatic error analysis with corrected SQL)
docs.queryPanel1: **Panel Position** - Located at the bottom, occupies 50% of vertical space. Split with SQL Editor (50% each).
docs.queryPanel2: **Auto-Scroll** - When new results arrive, the panel automatically scrolls to show them.
docs.queryPanel3: **Result Order** - New results appear at the top of the list (most recent first).
docs.queryPanel4: **Horizontal Scrolling** - Each result table is horizontally scrollable if there are more columns than can be displayed.
docs.queryPanel5: **SQL Display** - Each result shows the SQL that was executed above the table.
docs.queryPanel6: **Download CSV Button** - Download the result table as a CSV file.
docs.queryPanel7: **Expand/Collapse** - Each result can be expanded/collapsed to save space.
docs.queryPanel8: **Timestamp** - Shows when each query was executed.
docs.tooltipSelectDb: Choose the database for this chat session
docs.tooltipDbSelect: Select the database connection to use
docs.tooltipNewChat: Start a new conversation with the AI
docs.tooltipRenameChat: Give this chat a meaningful name
docs.tooltipCollapsePanel: Hide this panel to get more space
docs.tooltipSendMessage: Send your question to the AI
docs.tooltipAllowModifications: When enabled, AI can run INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE (asks for confirmation)
docs.tooltipViewSql: See the SQL query that was generated
docs.tooltipDownloadCsv: Download these results as CSV file
docs.tooltipRemoveResult: Remove this result from the list
docs.schemaLevelDesc: Provides a high-level interactive map of all schemas in your database. Uses D3.js to visually group tables by schema.
docs.tableLevelDesc: Interactive force-directed graph showing tables as nodes and foreign keys as links. Supports auto-layout, dragging nodes, and zooming to explore complex relationships.
docs.columnLevelDesc: Deep dive into column-level dependencies. visually traces how specific columns reference others across tables.
docs.visTip1: Drag nodes to rearrange the layout
docs.visTip2: Zoom in/out using your mouse wheel
docs.visTip3: Click on a node to highlight its connections
docs.visTip4: The graph automatically arranges for optimal viewing
docs.visTip5: The graph automatically switches level (Schema -> Table -> Column) based on your selection.
docs.visTip6: Use the 'Filter' option to focus on specific schemas or tables.
docs.visTip7: Clicking a node centers the view and highlights connected relationships.
docs.visUI1: **Connection Dropdown** - Select which database to visualize.
docs.visUI2: **Schema Dropdown** - Filter to a specific schema or leave empty for all.
docs.visUI3: **Table Dropdown** - Select a specific table to focus on its relationships.
docs.visUI4: **Column Dropdown** - (When table selected) Choose a column to see column-level dependencies.
docs.visUI5: **Show All Columns Checkbox** - Display all columns in table nodes, not just key columns.
docs.visUI6: **Show Only Connected Tables** - Hide isolated tables with no relationships.
docs.visUI7: **Object Types Checkboxes** - Toggle visibility: Tables, Views, Procedures, Functions, Triggers, Sequences, Materialized Views.
docs.visUI8: **Generate Visualization Button** - Renders the graph based on your selections.
docs.visUI9: **Zoom In/Out Buttons** - Adjust the zoom level of the graph.
docs.visUI10: **Graph Canvas** - Interactive area: drag nodes, scroll to zoom, click to focus.
docs.visUI11: **Edge Labels** - Show relationship descriptions (e.g., foreign key column names).
docs.visUI12: **Table Nodes** - Display table name, columns (with types), and key icons.
docs.tooltipVisSchema: Filter to show only tables from this schema
docs.tooltipVisTable: Focus on this table and its relationships
docs.tooltipShowAllColumns: Show all columns in table nodes, not just keys
docs.tooltipConnectedOnly: Hide tables that have no relationships
docs.tooltipObjectTypes: Toggle visibility of different object types
docs.tooltipGenerateVis: Generate the visualization based on your selections
docs.tooltipResetZoom: Reset zoom to fit all content
docs.importStep2: Choose your file (CSV, Excel .xlsx/.xls, SQL dump, or JSON)
docs.importStep3: Map columns to your target table (for CSV/Excel/JSON)
docs.importStep5: Execute the import and verify results
docs.importWarningText: Always backup your data before large imports. Consider testing with a small subset first.
docs.importUI1: **Connection Dropdown** - Select the database where you want to import data.
docs.importUI2: **File Upload Area** - Drag & drop a file or click to browse. Supports CSV, Excel, SQL, JSON.
docs.importUI3: **Schema Dropdown** - Choose the schema where the target table is located.
docs.importUI4: **Table Dropdown** - Select an existing table to import into.
docs.importUI5: **Create New Table Toggle** - Enable to create a new table from your file structure.
docs.importUI6: **New Table Name Field** - (When creating) Enter the name for the new table.
docs.importUI7: **Parsing Settings Button** - Configure: encoding (UTF-8, Latin-1), delimiter (comma, semicolon, tab), header row.
docs.importUI8: **Column Mapping Table** - Map each file column to a database column.
docs.importUI9: **Column Type Dropdown** - (When creating) Select data type: TEXT, INTEGER, REAL, DATE, BOOLEAN.
docs.importUI10: **Rename Column Input** - Change the column name before import.
docs.importUI11: **Hide Column Button** (eye icon) - Exclude a column from the import.
docs.importUI12: **Duplicate Handling Dropdown** - Choose: 'Error' (fail on duplicates), 'Skip' (ignore), 'Update' (overwrite existing).
docs.importUI13: **Preview Section** - Shows first 5 rows of your file before importing.
docs.importUI14: **Import Button** - Executes the import with your configured settings.
docs.importUI15: **Import History Section** - View past imports with details: filename, rows imported/failed, timestamp.
docs.tooltipImportConnection: Select the database where you want to import data
docs.tooltipImportSchema: Choose the schema containing the target table
docs.tooltipImportTable: Select or create the table to import into
docs.tooltipFileUpload: Drag & drop or click to select a file (CSV, Excel, SQL, JSON)

docs.tooltipClearResults: Remove all query results from the panel
docs.tooltipDownloadCsv: Download these results as a CSV file
docs.tooltipViewSql: Show the SQL query that was executed
docs.tooltipCollapsePanel: Collapse this panel to save space
docs.tooltipTablePreview: Click to preview first 10 rows of this table
docs.tooltipViewCode: Click to view the source code definition
docs.tooltipConnDatabase: Name of the specific database to connect to

docs.tooltipConnSchema: Default schema to use (e.g., 'public' for PostgreSQL)
docs.tooltipConnTest: Verify your connection settings before saving
docs.tooltipConnSave: Save this connection for future use
docs.tooltipVisSchema: Filter to a specific schema (leave empty for all)
docs.tooltipVisTable: Focus on a specific table's relationships
docs.tooltipVisColumn: Show column-level dependencies for this table
docs.tooltipVisAllColumns: Show all columns in table nodes, not just keys
docs.tooltipVisConnectedOnly: Hide tables that have no relationships
docs.tooltipVisGenerate: Render the dependency graph with current settings
docs.tooltipVisReset: Reset view to fit all nodes
docs.tooltipImportSchema: Choose the schema containing your target table
docs.tooltipImportTable: Select existing table or create new one
docs.tooltipImportCreateNew: Create a new table from your file structure
docs.tooltipImportSettings: Configure file parsing: encoding, delimiter, headers
docs.tooltipImportColumnType: Select data type for this column
docs.tooltipImportDuplicates: How to handle duplicate key conflicts
docs.tooltipImportExecute: Start importing data into the database
docs.securityFeature1Desc: All database connections support SSL/TLS encryption to protect your data in transit.
docs.securityFeature2Desc: Your database credentials are encrypted and never logged or exposed.
docs.securityFeature3Desc: Data modifications are disabled by default. You must explicitly enable them.
docs.securityFeature4Desc: Any data-modifying query requires explicit confirmation before execution.
docs.securityTipText: Use database accounts with minimal required permissions. For read-only analysis, use a read-only database user.
docs.tip1Content: Don't try to write SQL in the chat. Just describe what you want in plain language, and the AI will generate the optimal query.
docs.tip2Content: Use the Schema Browser to understand your database structure before asking complex questions.
docs.tip3Content: Test and save your database connections. This speeds up your workflow significantly.
docs.tip4Content: Always review the generated SQL and results. The AI is helpful but you know your data best.
docs.tip5Content: For databases with many tables, use the visualization feature to understand relationships quickly.
docs.tip6Content: Only enable data modifications when necessary, and always review the preview before confirming changes.
docs.tip7Content: Click on any table or view name in the Schema Browser sidebar to instantly run a 'SELECT * LIMIT 10' query and see its data.
docs.tip8Content: Left-click on procedures, functions, or triggers in the Schema Browser to view their source code definition in the editor. Right-click for context menu options.
docs.tip9Content: In the Query Results panel, you can press Ctrl+Enter (or Cmd+Enter) to quickly execute your manual SQL query.
docs.tip10Content: If your manual SQL fails, the AI automatically analyzes the error and provides a corrected query with a one-click 'Try again' button.
docs.tip11Content: Query results show 10 rows by default for speed. Use the 'Load more rows' button to fetch up to 1000 rows for deeper analysis.
docs.faq1Q: Is my database data stored anywhere?
docs.faq1A: No, we don't store your actual database data. Only connection metadata and chat history are saved. Query results are temporary and not persisted.
docs.faq2Q: Can I use this with production databases?
docs.faq2A: Yes, but we recommend using read-only credentials for production databases. This prevents accidental data modifications.
docs.faq3Q: What if the AI generates incorrect SQL?
docs.faq3A: You can always review the generated SQL before execution. If there's an error, the AI will help you fix it.
docs.faq4Q: How accurate is the AI for complex queries?
docs.faq4A: The AI works best with clear, specific questions. For very complex queries, you might need to break them into smaller parts.
docs.faq5Q: How does the diff view work?
docs.faq5A: When the AI modifies existing code or generates new SQL, changes are shown as a diff: red for deletions, green for additions. Use Keep to accept or Reject to discard. The diff appears both in the editor (inline) and in the chat (compact).
docs.faq6A: Yes, you can download any query results as CSV files directly from the Query Results Panel.
docs.faq7A: Free users have daily limits. Pro subscribers get unlimited queries and connections.
docs.faq8A: We currently support PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server.

footer.aboutDescription: Your intelligent database companion for seamless data exploration and analysis.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content: Welcome to SQLSphere. These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of our AI-powered database query and visualization platform. By using our service, you agree to be bound by these terms.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By accessing or using SQLSphere, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, please do not use our service.

serviceDescription.title: Service Description
serviceDescription.intro: SQLSphere provides an AI-powered platform for:

features.connections: Secure database connections (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server)
features.chat: Natural language queries translated to SQL
features.import: Data import from CSV, Excel, and other formats

items.registration: You must provide accurate and complete registration information
items.security: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
items.notification: You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized access
items.responsibility: You are responsible for all activities under your account

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions and Payments
subscriptions.intro: We offer the following subscription tiers:

free.name: Free Tier
free.description: Limited access with 1 connection, 3 messages/day, 2 lifetime imports

pro.name: Pro Tier
pro.description: Unlimited access at $25/month or $240/year (20% annual discount)

subscriptions.billing: Payments are processed through Stripe. Subscriptions renew automatically unless cancelled before the renewal date.

prohibited.illegal: Use the service for illegal purposes
prohibited.interfere: Interfere with or disrupt the service
prohibited.reverseEngineer: Reverse engineer or attempt to extract source code
prohibited.resell: Resell or redistribute the service without authorization
prohibited.malicious: Upload malicious code or harmful content

items.ownership: You retain ownership of all data in your connected databases
items.credentials: Database credentials are encrypted and stored securely
items.queries: Query history and chat sessions are stored to provide service functionality
items.responsibility: You are responsible for compliance with data protection laws applicable to your data

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.content: SQLSphere, including its software, design, and content, is protected by intellectual property laws. You may not copy, modify, or distribute our service without authorization.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
disclaimer.content: The service is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. We do not guarantee:

items.databaseChanges: That database modifications will produce intended results

limitation.title: Limitation of Liability
limitation.content: To the maximum extent permitted by law, SQLSphere shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including data loss or corruption, arising from your use of the service.

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.content: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless SQLSphere and its affiliates from any claims, damages, or expenses arising from your use of the service or violation of these terms.

termination.title: Termination
termination.content: We may suspend or terminate your access for violation of these terms. You may cancel your account at any time through your profile settings.

changes.title: Changes to Terms
changes.content: We may update these terms from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes via email or through the service. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

governingLaw.title: Governing Law
governingLaw.content: These terms are governed by the laws of [Your Jurisdiction]. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of [Your Jurisdiction].

contact.title: Contact Information
contact.content: For questions about these terms, please contact us:

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content: SQLSphere is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal information when you use our AI-powered database query platform.

items.name: Name (if provided)
items.oauth: OAuth provider data (Google, GitHub) if using social login

items.imports: Data import history (file names, row counts)

items.improve: Improve and optimize our AI models
items.communicate: Communicate with you about your account
items.security: Detect and prevent fraud and abuse

dataSecurity.title: Data Security
dataSecurity.intro: We implement robust security measures to protect your data:

items.encryption: AES-256 encryption for database credentials at rest
items.transit: TLS/SSL encryption for all data in transit
items.serverSide: Server-side credential handling (never exposed to client)
items.sessions: Session timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity

dataSharing.title: Data Sharing
dataSharing.intro: We do not sell your personal data. We may share data with:

legal.title: Legal Requirements
legal.content: When required by law or to protect our rights

items.account: Account data is retained while your account is active
items.chat: Chat history is retained for service functionality
items.logs: Technical logs are retained for 90 days
items.deletion: Upon account deletion, personal data is removed within 30 days

access.title: Access
access.content: Request a copy of your personal data

erasure.title: Erasure
erasure.content: Request deletion of your personal data

portability.title: Data Portability
portability.content: Receive your data in a structured format

international.title: International Data Transfers
international.content: Your data may be transferred to and processed in countries outside your residence. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place for such transfers.

children.title: Children's Privacy
children.content: Our service is not intended for children under 16. We do not knowingly collect data from children under 16.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of significant changes via email or through the service.

disputeResolution.title: EU Dispute Resolution
disputeResolution.euPlatform: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (OS):
disputeResolution.noParticipation: We are not obligated and not willing to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

liability.title: Liability for Content
liability.ownContent: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages according to general laws. However, we are not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information.
liability.externalLinks: Our offering contains links to external websites of third parties. We have no influence on their content. The respective provider or operator is always responsible for the content of linked pages.

copyright.title: Copyright
copyright.content: The content and works on these pages created by the site operators are subject to copyright law. Reproduction, editing, distribution, and any kind of use outside the limits of copyright law require written consent.

whatIs.title: What is SQLSphere?
whatIs.content: SQLSphere is an AI-powered platform that transforms how you interact with your databases. Instead of writing complex SQL queries, simply ask questions in natural language and get instant results. Our intelligent assistant understands your database structure and generates optimized queries for you.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: We believe everyone should be able to access and analyze their data without needing to be a SQL expert. Our mission is to democratize data access by making database interactions as simple as having a conversation.

supportedDatabases.title: Supported Databases
supportedDatabases.intro: We currently support the following database systems:

oracle.name: Oracle Database
oracle.note: Oracle Database support is planned for future releases.

connect.title: Connect Your Database
connect.description: Securely connect to your database using credentials or SSH tunneling. All credentials are encrypted.

ask.title: Ask Questions
ask.description: Type your questions in natural language. Our AI understands context and generates accurate SQL queries.

visualize.title: Visualize Structure
visualize.description: Explore your database schema with interactive visualizations showing tables, relationships, and dependencies.

manage.title: Import & Manage Data
manage.description: Import data from CSV, Excel, and other formats. Safely modify data with confirmation workflows.

security.title: Security First
security.intro: Your data security is our top priority:

items.noStorage: We never store your actual database data
items.confirmation: Two-step confirmation for all data modifications

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.content: Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you!

connections.title: Secure Connections
connections.description: Connect to PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server with encrypted credentials and SSH tunnel support.

chat.title: AI-Powered Chat
chat.description: Ask questions in natural language and get accurate SQL queries and results instantly.

visualization.title: Schema Visualization
visualization.description: Interactive graphs showing tables, relationships, and column dependencies.

import.title: Data Import
import.description: Import data from CSV, Excel, JSON, and SQL files with smart column mapping.

security.title: Enterprise Security
security.description: AES-256 encryption, server-side credential handling, and automatic session timeouts.

performance.title: Optimized Performance
performance.description: Fast query execution with intelligent caching and pagination.

errors.connectionError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.networkError: Connection failed. Please check your internet connection.
errors.authError: Session expired. Please log in again.
errors.notFoundError: The requested resource was not found.
errors.serverError: Server is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
errors.databaseError: Unable to connect to the database. Please check your connection settings.
errors.rateLimitError: Too many requests. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

form.title: Contact Us
form.description: Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you.
form.name: Your Name
form.subject: Subject
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us more about your inquiry...

success.title: Message Sent
success.description: Thank you for reaching out! We'll get back to you soon.

error.title: Failed to Send
error.description: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

shareDialog.title: Share Query Results
shareDialog.titlePlaceholder: Give your shared query a title

sharedView.expiredDesc: This shared query link has expired or does not exist.

schedules.title: Scheduled Reports
schedules.name: Name
schedules.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Show monthly revenue by region
schedules.previewRequired: Run preview before creating the report
schedules.reportDescriptionPlaceholder: Optional context sentence shown in the email
schedules.localNotSupported: This feature is not supported for local agent connections yet
schedules.csvAttachmentNote: Full result is attached as CSV.

alerts.title: Data Alerts
alerts.name: Name
alerts.conditionPlaceholder: e.g. When orders exceed 1000 in the last hour
alerts.sqlPlaceholder: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour' HAVING COUNT(*) > 1000
alerts.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Find customers with overdue invoices
alerts.previewRequired: Run preview before creating the alert
alerts.localNotSupported: This feature is not supported for local agent connections yet

dashboards.title: Dashboards
dashboards.name: Name
dashboards.widgetPrompt: Describe what you want to see
dashboards.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Show monthly revenue as a bar chart
dashboards.emptyDesc: Create your first dashboard to visualize your data with AI-generated charts.
dashboards.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this dashboard? All its widgets will also be removed.
